summary: Documented fixed-XOR-recurring invariant (leg 999.867) in TASK-PROPERTIES.md (corrected orthogonality note + XOR subsection) and TASK-STATE-MATRIX.md (invariant section + footnote caveat on Recurrence/Fixed cell).

findings.claim: Corrected inaccurate orthogonality note: 'any mode can be recurring' → 'any mode EXCEPT fixed may be recurring'; note now points to new XOR subsection.
findings.evidence: docs/architecture/TASK-PROPERTIES.md — orthogonality note updated; new 'Fixed–Recurring Exclusion (XOR invariant)' subsection added under Scheduling Modes.

findings.claim: Added 'Fixed–Recurring Exclusion (XOR invariant)' subsection: exact invariant definition, isFixedRecurringConflict cite at taskValidation.js:98, all 4 chokepoints, invalid_combination outcome, flip-handling note, leg 999.867 reference.
findings.evidence: docs/architecture/TASK-PROPERTIES.md — new subsection between Scheduling Modes note and Status Effects section.

findings.claim: Added 'Fixed–Recurring XOR Invariant' section after Regular Task Scheduling Modes: exact invariant statement, taskValidation.js:98 citation, 4-chokepoint table, invalid_combination outcome, leg 999.867 reference.
findings.evidence: docs/architecture/TASK-STATE-MATRIX.md — new section inserted before Drag-to-Pin State Machine.

findings.claim: Added footnote ¹ caveat to 🔁 Recurrence / Fixed cell in Field Visibility Matrix → Regular Tasks: backend rejects combination server-side; frontend control-visibility explicitly stated as NOT verified by this leg.
findings.evidence: docs/architecture/TASK-STATE-MATRIX.md — cell changed to '✅ ¹'; footnote block added below table.

findings.claim: Regular Task Scheduling Modes section still uses legacy 'when = "fixed"' terminology; current code uses placement_mode = 'fixed'. Out of scope for leg 999.894.
findings.evidence: TASK-STATE-MATRIX.md lines 169–173 reference 'when = "fixed"'; TASK-PROPERTIES.md and code use placement_mode.
findings.fix: Future chore: update Regular Task Scheduling Modes section to use placement_mode terminology throughout.

refers.why: verify accuracy and completeness of authored XOR invariant sections in both docs
